Best for\n211\t        hermetic tests.\n212\t\n213\t        ``via=\"dispatch\"``: runs the full router → fetch+synthesize pipeline via\n214\t        ``POST /api/v1/test/dispatch`` (requires the ZAI LLM proxy). ``utterance``\n215\t        is required for this path; ``label`` is ignored (the router derives it),\n216\t        so it may be omitted.\n217\t        \"\"\"\n218\t        session_id = self._resolve_session_id(session_id)\n219\t        if via == \"direct\":\n220\t            if not label:\n221\t                raise ValueError(\"via='direct' requires a 'label'\")\n222\t            payload = {\n223\t                \"session_id\": session_id,\n224\t                \"label\": label,\n225\t                \"type\": topic_type,\n226\t                \"summary\": summary,\n227\t                \"urgency\": urgency,\n228\t                \"staleness_seconds\": staleness_seconds,\n229\t            }\n230\t            resp = await self._client.post(\"/api/v1/test/create-topic\", json=payload)\n231\t            self._check(resp)\n232\t            return resp.json()\n233\t        elif via == \"dispatch\":\n234\t            if not utterance:\n235\t                raise ValueError(\"via='dispatch' requires an 'utterance'\")\n236\t            payload = {\n237\t                \"utterance\": utterance,\n238\t                \"session_id\": session_id,\n239\t                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n240\t                \"wait_for_results\": wait_for_results,\n241\t                \"timeout_seconds\": timeout_seconds,\n242\t            }\n243\t            resp = await self._client.post(\"/api/v1/test/dispatch\", json=payload)\n244\t            self._check(resp)\n245\t            return resp.json()\n246\t        raise ValueError(f\"unknown via={via!r} (expected 'direct' or 'dispatch')\")\n247\t\n248\t    async def inject_topics(\n249\t        self,\n250\t        session_id: str,\n251\t        specs: list[dict],\n252\t    ) -> list[dict]:\n253\t        \"\"\"Inject many topics sequentially. self._client = client\n        elif app is not None:\n            # ASGI transport drives the app directly — no socket, no server.\n            self._client = httpx.AsyncClient(\n                transport=httpx.ASGITransport(app=app),\n                base_url=_TESTSERVER,\n                timeout=timeout,\n            )\n        else:\n            self._client = httpx.AsyncClient(base_url=base_url, timeout=timeout)\n\n    # -- lifecycle -----------------------------------------------------------\n\n    async def __aenter__(self) -> \"TestDataInjector\":\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -> None:\n        await self.aclose()\n\n    async def aclose(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clean up every tracked session, then close the owned httpx client.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            await self.cleanup()\n        finally:\n            if self._owns_client:\n                await self._client.aclose()\n\n    # -- helpers -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def predictable_session_id(label: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build a stable, prefixed session ID from a human label.\"\"\"\n        safe = \"\".join(c if c.isalnum() or c in \"-_\" else \"-\" for c in label).strip(\"-\")\n        return f\"{TEST_SESSION_PREFIX}{safe or 'session'}\"\n\n    @classmethod\n    def _resolve_session_id(cls, session_id: Optional[str]) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Normalize any session-id input into a prefixed, trackable test ID.\n\n        Guarantees every session this injector touches carries the test prefix\n        (so it is easy to spot and bulk-clean, and never collides with a real\n        production session id):\n\n        - ``None``          → auto-generate ``test-inject-<hex>``\n        - already-prefixed  → returned verbatim (idempotent)\n        - anything else     → namespaced via :meth:`predictable_session_id`\n        \"\"\"\n        if session_id is None:\n            return f\"{TEST_SESSION_PREFIX}{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"\n        if session_id.startswith(TEST_SESSION_PREFIX):\n            return session_id\n        return cls.predictable_session_id(session_id)\n\n    def _track(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        if session_id not in self._created_session_ids:\n            self._created_session_ids.append(session_id)\n\n    def _untrack(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        if session_id in self._created_session_ids:\n            self._created_session_ids.remove(session_id)\n\n    @property\n    def tracked_sessions(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Session IDs this injector will clean up on teardown.\"\"\"\n        return list(self._created_session_ids)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _check(resp: httpx.Response, *, expected: int = 200) -> httpx.Response:\n        if resp.status_code != expected:\n            raise InjectionError(\n                f\"HTTP {resp.status_code} (expected {expected}): {resp.text[:300]}\"\n            )\n        return resp\n\n    # -- session -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    async def create_session(self, session_id: Optional[str] = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Create a test session. Auto-generates a prefixed ID if none given.\n\n        Explicit labels are namespaced under the test prefix (see\n        :meth:`_resolve_session_id`) so they stay easy to spot and bulk-clean.\n        Idempotent: calling twice with the same input returns ``created: false``\n        the second time. The resolved session is tracked for teardown.\n        \"\"\"\n        session_id = self._resolve_session_id(session_id)\n        resp = await self._client.post(\"/api/v1/sessions\", json={\"session_id\": session_id})\n        self._check(resp)\n        data = resp.json()\n        sid = data.get(\"session_id\", session_id)\n        self._track(sid)\n        return data\n\n    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Return the raw session row (via the store) or None if absent.\"\"\"\n        # There is no public GET /sessions/{id}; reuse the topics endpoint as a\n        # presence probe by checking the store directly through create-session\n        # idempotency: a session that already exists reports created=false.\n        session_id = self._resolve_session_id(session_id)\n        resp = await self._client.post(\"/api/v1/sessions\", json={\"session_id\": session_id})\n        self._check(resp)\n        data = resp.json()\n        return {\"session_id\": data[\"session_id\"], \"exists\": not data[\"created\"]}\n\n    async def delete_session(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Delete a session and all its data. Untracks it regardless of outcome.\"\"\"\n        session_id = self._resolve_session_id(session_id)\n        resp = await self._client.delete(f\"/api/v1/sessions/{session_id}\")\n        self._check(resp)\n        self._untrack(session_id)\n        return resp.json()\n\n    # -- topics --------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    async def inject_topic(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        *,\n        label: Optional[str] = None,\n        topic_type: str = \"project\",\n        summary: str = \"injected test result\",\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n        staleness_seconds: int = 0,\n        via: str = \"direct\",\n        utterance: Optional[str] = None,\n        surface_id: Optional[str] = None,\n        wait_for_results: bool = True,\n        timeout_seconds: int = 30,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Inject one topic into ``session_id``.\n\n        ``via=\"direct\"`` (default): writes a topic + result straight to the store\n        via ``POST /api/v1/test/create-topic`` — deterministic, no LLM. Best for\n        hermetic tests.\n\n        ``via=\"dispatch\"``: runs the full router → fetch+synthesize pipeline via\n        ``POST /api/v1/test/dispatch`` (requires the ZAI LLM proxy). ``utterance``\n        is required for this path; ``label`` is ignored (the router derives it),\n        so it may be omitted.\n        \"\"\"\n        session_id = self._resolve_session_id(session_id)\n        if via == \"direct\":\n            if not label:\n                raise ValueError(\"via='direct' requires a 'label'\")\n            payload = {\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n                \"label\": label,\n                \"type\": topic_type,\n                \"summary\": summary,\n                \"urgency\": urgency,\n                \"staleness_seconds\": staleness_seconds,\n            }\n            resp = await self._client.post(\"/api/v1/test/create-topic\", json=payload)\n            self._check(resp)\n            return resp.json()\n        elif via == \"dispatch\":\n            if not utterance:\n                raise ValueError(\"via='dispatch' requires an 'utterance'\")\n            payload = {\n                \"utterance\": utterance,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"wait_for_results\": wait_for_results,\n                \"timeout_seconds\": timeout_seconds,\n            }\n            resp = await self._client.post(\"/api/v1/test/dispatch\", json=payload)\n            self._check(resp)\n            return resp.json()\n        raise ValueError(f\"unknown via={via!r} (expected 'direct' or 'dispatch')\")\n\n    async def inject_topics(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        specs: list[dict],\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Inject many topics sequentially. Each spec is a kwarg dict for inject_topic.\"\"\"\n        results = []\n        for spec in specs:\n            results.append(await self.inject_topic(session_id, **spec))\n        return results\n\n    async def inject_session_with_topics(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_specs: list[dict],\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convenience: create a session, then inject N topics into it.\"\"\"\n        sid = self._resolve_session_id(session_id)\n        await self.create_session(sid)\n        topics = await self.inject_topics(sid, topic_specs)\n        return {\"session_id\": sid, \"topic_count\": len(topics), \"topics\": topics}\n\n    async def get_topics(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Return the canvas topic cards for a session (GET .../topics).\"\"\"\n        session_id = self._resolve_session_id(session_id)\n        resp = await self._client.get(f\"/api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics\")\n        self._check(resp)\n        data = resp.json()\n        return data.get(\"cards\", [])\n\n    # -- teardown ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    async def cleanup(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Delete every tracked session. Best-effort: never raises.\n\n        Returns the list of session IDs actually removed.\n        \"\"\"\n        removed: list[str] = []\n        for sid in list(self._created_session_ids):\n            try:\n                await self.delete_session(sid)\n                removed.append(sid)\n            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — teardown must be best-effort\n                logger.warning(f\"cleanup: failed to delete session {sid}: {exc}\")\n                self._untrack(sid)\n        return removed\n","numLines":294,"startLine":1,"totalLines":294}}}

These tests lock down its contract *without* needing a live server\n7\tor a browser:\n8\t\n9\t- They drive the real FastAPI app in-process via httpx's ``ASGITransport`` (no\n10\t  socket, no ``uvicorn``), so they always run in CI.\n11\t- They point the session store at an isolated tmp SQLite file (via\n12\t  ``ADC_DB_PATH`` + a reset of the store singleton) so they never read or write\n13\t  the production ``data/session.db``.\n14\t\n15\tWhat this suite locks down (the \"inject + clean up, hermetically\" contract):\n16\t\n17\t1. **Session creation** — predictable prefixed tests for the canvas test-data injection utilities (bead adc-5unt).\n\n``TestDataInjector`` (tests/e2e/inject.py) is the async httpx client that the\nPlaywright/browser canvas suites use to set up known sessions + topics and tear\nthem down. These tests lock down its contract *without* needing a live server\nor a browser:\n\n- They drive the real FastAPI app in-process via httpx's ``ASGITransport`` (no\n  socket, no ``uvicorn``), so they always run in CI.\n- They point the session store at an isolated tmp SQLite file (via\n  ``ADC_DB_PATH`` + a reset of the store singleton) so they never read or write\n  the production ``data/session.db``.\n\nWhat this suite locks down (the \"inject + clean up, hermetically\" contract):\n\n1. **Session creation** — predictable prefixed IDs, auto-generated IDs, and\n   idempotency (created=True the first time, False on a repeat).\n2. **Topic injection** — a session can be injected with multiple topics that are\n   all readable back through the same GET /topics endpoint the canvas uses.\n3. **Cleanup** — every tracked session is deleted on ``aclose()``/``cleanup()``,\n   removing its topics and results; ``async with`` teardown runs even on error.\n4. **Isolation** — injected rows land in the tmp DB, never in the production\n   session.db.\n\"\"\"\nimport aiosqlite\nimport pytest\n\nimport src.main as main_mod\nimport src.session.store as store_mod\nfrom src.topic.model import TopicManager\nfrom tests.e2e.inject import (\n    DEFAULT_BASE_URL,\n    TEST_SESSION_PREFIX,\n    TestDataInjector,\n)\n\n# The production store path — used *only* to assert monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"An in-process TestDataInjector backed by an ISOLATED tmp DB.\n\n    - Sets ``ADC_DB_PATH`` to a tmp file and resets the store singleton so the\n      app's store is recreated at that path (never ``data/session.db``).\n    - Wires ``main._topic_manager`` so GET /sessions/{id}/topics works without\n      the full app lifespan (ASGITransport does not run lifespan startup).\n    - On teardown: cleans up every tracked session via the injector, closes the\n      httpx client, and restores the real singletons + env.\n    \"\"\"\n    tmp_db = tmp_path / \"test-session.db\"\n    monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_DB_PATH\", str(tmp_db))\n\n    # Save the process-wide singletons so other tests are unaffected.\n    saved_store_singleton = store_mod._store\n    saved_main_store = main_mod._store\n    saved_topic_manager = main_mod._topic_manager\n\n    # Force get_store() to rebuild at the tmp path on next call.\n    store_mod._store = None\n    main_mod._store = None\n    main_mod._topic_manager = None\n\n    store = store_mod.get_store()  # reads ADC_DB_PATH → tmp_db\n    await store.initialize()\n    main_mod._topic_manager = TopicManager(store)\n\n    async with TestDataInjector(app=main_mod.app) as injector:\n        yield injector, tmp_db\n\n    # Restore — never leak the tmp store into the rest of the process.\n    main_mod._topic_manager = saved_topic_manager\n    main_mod._store = saved_main_store\n    store_mod._store = saved_store_singleton\n\n\nasync def _count(db_path, sql, args=()):\n    \"\"\"Run a scalar COUNT(*) query against db_path; return the integer.\"\"\"\n    async with aiosqlite.connect(db_path) as db:\n        async with db.execute(sql, args) as cur:\n            row = await cur.fetchone()\n            return int(row[0]) if row else 0\n\n\n# --- 1. session creation ---------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestSessionCreation:\n    \"\"\"create_session() honors explicit IDs,
